title: Body Scan
blurb: Move attention slowly through the body to release tension and reconnect.
lead: The body scan builds present-moment awareness by moving attention gently through the body, part by part — a cornerstone of MBSR.

blocks.text: A body scan involves moving your attention slowly through your body, noticing sensations without trying to change them.
It builds interoception (awareness of internal sensations), releases held tension, and anchors you in the present. It's a core practice in Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR).

items: Lie down or sit comfortably. Close your eyes.
Bring attention to your feet — notice any sensations: warmth, pressure, tingling, or nothing at all.
Slowly move upward — legs, hips, belly, chest, arms, hands, neck, face.
If your mind wanders or you feel nothing in an area, that's fine — just move on gently.
Finish by sensing the whole body breathing.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Stress, tension, sleep, reconnecting with the body

blocks.text: Trying to force relaxation. The goal is awareness, not a particular state — relaxation often follows on its own.
